_meta.purpose: Externalizes the Lead-to-POS matching business rules so the engine reads them from config instead of hardcoding. Values below reflect the CURRENT behavior in the code; the 'divergences' block lists where today's code does not yet match this contract.

grounded_in.six_set_and_closed_existing: lead_matching.py (NOT wired into the active pipeline)

_meta.primary_fact: A LEAD can match MANY POS transactions (one-to-many). A POS transaction belongs to AT MOST ONE lead (one-to-one): when several leads score against the same pos_id, the highest similarity_score wins and the others are dropped. Among the POS a lead matches, the earliest qualifying ('Complete') transaction by fiscal_year -> fiscal_period -> week is flagged primary_transaction.
divergences_current_code_vs_this_contrac: Leads are still embedded with text-embedding-005 (vector_db_loading_leads.py); this contract requires gemini-embedding-001 on BOTH sides. Until fixed, cross-model cosine is invalid.
Leads are not L2-normalized and still write a zero-vector fallback; this contract requires l2_normalize=true and drop-on-failure.
Confidence bands are currently read from the lead_mgmt_*.match_configuration DB table, not from code; this JSON proposes moving them here. Keep ONE source of truth.
The validation plan mentions embedding OMS address fields, but the embedders only embed combined_field / full_address / business_name. OMS fields feed the deterministic (exact) matcher only.
Schema is hardcoded as lead_mgmt_costco in the matching SQL; 'environment.schema' below is the value the SQL must be parameterized with.

scope._warehouse_options: integer = single warehouse | [int,int,...] = subset | null = all warehouses
scope._fiscal_year_note: null = bind :fiscal_year_sales at runtime from current Costco fiscal info

candidate_constraints.fiscal_expression: lead.fiscal_year < pos.fiscal_year OR (lead.fiscal_year = pos.fiscal_year AND lead.fiscal_period <= pos.fiscal_period)

candidate_retrieval._gate_note: Keep candidate only if (lead.combined_embedding <=> pos.combined_embedding) <= 0.2, i.e. cosine similarity >= 0.80.
candidate_retrieval._combined_score_note: Weighted average; divisor 8 = sum of weights (1+4+3). Range 0-100.

confidence_bands._note: Applied to combined_score (0-100). Inclusive lower bound, exclusive upper bound except the top band.

primary_transaction.definition: Earliest qualifying transaction per lead is flagged primary_transaction = true.

closed_existing._status: Implemented in lead_matching.py (six-set engine); NOT active in the wired fuzzy path.
closed_existing.rule: If a matched transaction predates the lead (earlier fiscal_year, or same year earlier period, or same year/period earlier week), mark the lead Closed-Existing, remove it from the active matching set, and stop matching it for the rest of the run.

exact_match_reference._note: Deterministic exact matcher that runs before/alongside semantic. Kept here so both layers share one rule file.

override_policy.rule: Semantic (fuzzy) result overrides the exact result for a lead ONLY when fuzzy similarity_score is strictly greater than the exact similarity_score.
